topic: Correlating Human Memory Tricks with LLM Memory
key_insights: Chunking in human working memory is directly analogous to how LLM data pipelines segment text into vector embeddings.
Foer's concept of transactive/external memory (remembering 'where' the info is rather than the info itself) is exactly how Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) operates.
The Memory Palace technique, organizing info spatially, mirrors Vector Databases plotting semantic similarity in multi-dimensional space.
next_steps: Draft blog content based on user-approved outline.
